<!-- generated by spiralspec v0.1.0 — do not edit; see docs to customize -->

# SpiralSpec — Refine

The full-circle feedback loop. The user has reviewed the plan (or the spec
itself) and wants changes that go deeper than expanding tasks: reshaping
criteria, context, the ledger, or tasks that have not started. Specs are
living artifacts — this is the verb that keeps them alive before and between
implementation runs.

## What may change here

- `backlog.md` — add newly discovered entries, edit goals, delete entries the
  user discards (living inventory).
- Not-started tasks (`status: backlog` or `todo`) — edit anything: goal,
  ground, scope, body sections. Delete them entirely (remove the file AND its
  ledger entry) when the user discards the work.
- Tasks in `inprogress` — editable too, but the change IS a new iteration:
  append an `# Iterations` entry prefixed with `(refined — pending)` describing
  what changed and why (goal, scope, or task-list delta). The implementing
  agent re-reads the task file before acting, so the iteration is picked up
  mid-flight; it will drop the pending marker and append its own progress entry
  when it acts on the instruction. Never edit silently.
- `acceptance-criteria.md` and `context.md` — propose concrete edits when the
  feedback reveals the spec itself was wrong or incomplete; apply them only
  with the user's explicit consent (human-owned artifacts).
- `solution.md` — same consent rule.

## What may NOT change here

- Tasks in `verification`, `release`, or `done` — feedback on implemented
  work goes through /spiral:verify (which bounds the blast radius with
  `spiralspec impact`).
- Task statuses — approval flips (`backlog → todo`) are the user's, and
  execution flips belong to /spiral:implement.

## Steps

1. Run `spiralspec status <spec> --json`. Ask what the feedback is if not
   already given.
2. Classify each feedback item: ledger change, task edit/delete, or spec-text
   change (criteria/context/solution). Reject items that touch started tasks —
   point the user to /spiral:verify instead.
3. When a criteria or context change is requested: identify which ledger
   entries and not-started tasks it invalidates or reshapes, present the full
   set of consequences, and get consent before touching the human-owned files.
4. Apply the changes. Scope edits are normal here — the claims of not-started
   tasks are re-plannable at will.
5. Run `spiralspec validate <spec>` until it prints OK (ledger consistency,
   scope overlap, ground refs — the mechanical net for everything you just
   moved).
6. Update `status/README.md` (`# Current state`, `# Next steps`) so a reader
   who was not in this conversation understands what changed and why.
7. Suggest the natural next verb: /spiral:plan to expand new entries,
   /spiral:implement to build.

## Rules

- Re-read files before editing them; the user may have changed artifacts
  since the last read.
- Never regenerate wholesale — apply the user's feedback surgically and
  preserve everything else.
- Deleting a task requires the user to name it (or confirm your proposal);
  never delete on inference.
